text:
Artificial intelligence is increasingly common in modern workplaces. Many companies use AI for automation, handling repetitive tasks like data entry or scheduling. This frees up employees to focus on more creative and strategic work. Productivity tools powered by AI also help workers analyze information faster, generate reports, and communicate more efficiently.

However, AI is not replacing humans entirely. Instead, human-AI collaboration is becoming the norm. Workers use AI as an assistant, making decisions based on its suggestions while applying their own judgment and expertise.

These changes naturally affect jobs. Some roles evolve, requiring new digital skills, while others may disappear entirely. To succeed, professionals must adapt by learning to work alongside AI and developing abilities that machines cannot easily replicate, such as emotional intelligence and complex problem-solving.
